summary: Although the Biostatistician role offers the predictable daytime hours and low burnout risk you prioritize, it is a poor fit due to your struggle with complex mathematics and logical reasoning. The position demands extraordinary quantitative reasoning and mastery of advanced calculus, which directly conflicts with your preference for procedural, protocol-driven work. Entering a career that requires your least-developed skills would likely cause extreme cognitive stress, negating the benefits of the excellent work-life balance.
